ideas.title: The Weightless Teacup
ideas.description: A porcelain teacup floating mid-air, steam curling upward into tiny constellations. The saucer hovers below it, casting a moon-shaped shadow.

ideas.title: Rain That Forgets to Fall
ideas.description: Raindrops suspended in the air, each one holding a tiny reflection of a different world inside it. One drop holds a forest, another a city, another a single candle flame.

ideas.title: Fox Who Collects Sunsets
ideas.description: A small fox with a satchel made of clouds, walking along a hilltop collecting jars of golden sunset light. Each jar glows a different shade.

ideas.title: Library at the Bottom of the Ocean
ideas.description: Ancient bookshelves stretching into dark blue water, fish swimming between the spines. One open book has pages that are actually coral growing outward.

ideas.title: Girl Made of Autumn Leaves
ideas.description: A silhouette of a girl, but her body is composed entirely of falling autumn leaves in amber, crimson and gold. Wind catches the edges of her, and she begins to drift.

ideas.title: Moon's Breakfast
ideas.description: The moon sitting on a cloud, eating starlight from a bowl with a spoon made of a comet's tail. A small owl waits beside it for crumbs.

ideas.title: Invisible Garden
ideas.description: An empty garden plot with only shadows of flowers visible on the ground — the flowers themselves are transparent, only their shadows prove they exist.

ideas.title: Cloud Shepherd
ideas.description: A child floating on the back of a giant cloud, using a long stick to herd smaller clouds like fluffy sheep across the blue sky.

ideas.title: The Lantern Fish Market
ideas.description: Underwater vendors selling glowing deep-sea creatures like goods at a night market. Anglerfish hang as lanterns. Jellyfish float as chandeliers.

ideas.title: Hands Full of Weather
ideas.description: Two open palms held up — one hand holds a tiny thunderstorm, the other holds a sunrise. The raindrops from one hand drift toward the light of the other.

ideas.title: Cat Café on the Moon
ideas.description: A cozy little café on the lunar surface, cats floating gently in low gravity between tables. Earth hangs large in the window like a painting.

ideas.title: Staircase to the Wind
ideas.description: A spiral staircase in an open field that leads nowhere, but at the top, the air shimmers and ribbons of color ripple outward like invisible wings.

ideas.title: Whale in the Wheat Field
ideas.description: A blue whale swimming slowly through a golden wheat field at dusk, its shadow long and cool across the ground below, birds circling around its fins.

ideas.title: The Girl Who Knitted Stars
ideas.description: A woman in a rocking chair on a dark porch, knitting a scarf made of actual starlight. Each star plucked from the sky leaves a small dark hole she later patches with moonbeams.

ideas.title: Forest of Frozen Music
ideas.description: Trees whose branches are made of crystallized musical notes. When the wind blows, they chime softly. Sheet music grows from the roots like mushrooms.

ideas.title: Tiny Planet Bedroom
ideas.description: A small spherical planet just big enough for a bedroom — bed, lamp, bookshelf curving with the planet's surface. Stars close enough to touch from the window.

ideas.title: Map of Emotions
ideas.description: A hand-drawn map where each country is an emotion — the mountains are made of old memories, rivers are tears, deserts are numbness, forests are joy.

ideas.title: Butterfly Who Collects Shadows
ideas.description: A large butterfly with wings made of translucent glass, collecting the shadows of flowers and pinning them gently inside its wings like specimens.

ideas.title: Clockmaker's Dream
ideas.description: A sleeping clockmaker, and above her head all the gears and hands she ever worked on float in a dream-cloud, ticking softly, forming the shape of a bird mid-flight.

ideas.title: Letters That Never Arrived
ideas.description: A pile of sealed envelopes floating upward into a night sky, each one glowing faintly with the warmth of the words inside. Fireflies mistake them for lanterns.

ideas.title: River That Runs Uphill
ideas.description: A river flowing upward against gravity, defying physics, its fish swimming happily upward too. At the top it breaks into a waterfall of stars.

ideas.title: The Bread Baker's Sun
ideas.description: A baker who bakes the sun each morning — rolling it out like dough, dusting it with golden flour, letting it rise before sliding it into the sky.

ideas.title: Treehouse at the Edge of a Dream
ideas.description: A treehouse perched in a tree that grows at the very border between sky and dream. One side is daytime blue, the other is deep purple night. The ladder goes both ways.

ideas.title: Glass Mountain Village
ideas.description: A village built on a translucent glass mountain — you can see the roots of all the houses reaching down through the glass, and the clouds pass through it from inside.

ideas.title: Dandelion Universe
ideas.description: A single dandelion whose seeds, when blown, each become a tiny galaxy spinning gently through the air before landing and blooming into new stars.

ideas.title: The Cartographer of Clouds
ideas.description: An old man on a rooftop with a drafting table, carefully mapping each cloud as it passes — naming them, measuring them, marking their drift in an enormous leather journal.

ideas.title: Island That Only Appears at Night
ideas.description: An island that emerges from the ocean only after midnight — made of soft dark stone, lit by bioluminescent plants. By sunrise it sinks again, leaving only ripples.

ideas.title: Jelly Architecture
ideas.description: Buildings in a city made entirely of translucent jelly — they wobble slightly in the breeze, cast rainbow light on the street below, and make soft sounds when touched.

ideas.title: Paper Crane Migration
ideas.description: Thousands of origami paper cranes flying south in a V-formation like real birds, their folded wings catching the light differently at each angle, each one a different soft color.

ideas.title: The Painter Who Painted Weather
ideas.description: An artist standing before a blank sky with a huge brush, painting storm clouds in one corner, sunshine in another, and leaving the middle white and uncertain.

ideas.title: Sleeping Giant's Garden
ideas.description: A giant who has been asleep for centuries, now covered in moss and wildflowers. Rabbits nest in their palms. A small river runs along their collarbone.

ideas.title: Constellation Puppets
ideas.description: A puppeteer using strings of starlight to animate constellation figures — Orion dances, Cassiopeia waves, the Big Dipper pours glittering dust.

ideas.title: Kitchen Full of Impossible Ingredients
ideas.description: A kitchen counter with ingredients like: a jar of Mondays, a stick of morning dew, powdered starlight, essence of first laugh, and a pinch of forgotten dreams.

ideas.title: The Fog Architect
ideas.description: A figure who sculpts fog into temporary shapes — bridges, archways, staircases — that stand for minutes before dissolving. Others cross them while they last.

ideas.title: Tide Pool Universe
ideas.description: A tiny tide pool that, when viewed from above, looks exactly like a galaxy — the sea anemones are star clusters, the pebbles are planets, the trapped crab is a comet.

ideas.title: Spiral Staircase of Seasons
ideas.description: A spiral staircase where each floor is a different season — step up from snowy winter, through spring blossoms, to summer sun, to autumn gold, then back to winter.

ideas.title: Lighthouse in a Storm of Stars
ideas.description: A lighthouse standing not by an ocean but in the middle of a meteor shower — its beam sweeping through falling stars, guiding something home from deep space.

ideas.title: The Emotion Aquarium
ideas.description: An aquarium where the fish represent emotions — a fast silver fish is anxiety, a slow golden one is contentment, a tiny glowing one is hope, a dark shadow is grief.

ideas.title: Midnight Greenhouse
ideas.description: A greenhouse open only at night, growing plants that bloom only in darkness — black roses, phosphorescent ferns, flowers that sing softly at 3am.

ideas.title: The Memory Weaver
ideas.description: An elder weaving a tapestry where each thread is a memory — you can see faces, places and moments woven into the fabric. Some threads are bright. Some are fraying.

ideas.title: Cloud Architecture Studio
ideas.description: Clouds shaped like unfinished buildings — columns that rise but don't support anything, arches in mid-air, blueprints sketched in cirrus against the blue.

ideas.title: Bee-keeper of Dreams
ideas.description: A bee-keeper whose hive produces not honey but dreams — golden, glowing jars of sleep collected each morning from the dreaming bees. The jars hum softly.

ideas.title: Paper Town in the Rain
ideas.description: A miniature town built of paper — houses, trees, tiny people — in the middle of a puddle, the rain creating ripples that make the whole town shimmer and warp.

ideas.title: The Last Archivist of Sunsets
ideas.description: An old woman cataloging sunsets in enormous leather-bound books — each page a pressed, preserved sunset, labeled with its date, location, and exact shade of rose.

ideas.title: Architect of Silence
ideas.description: A figure building walls out of silence — each brick a moment of quiet, cemented together with pauses. The finished room inside holds only the sound of breathing.

ideas.title: Origami Galaxies
ideas.description: Galaxies folded from rice paper — spiral arms neatly creased, stars punched through with a pin, held together with the geometry of careful hands.

ideas.title: The Cartography of Scars
ideas.description: A human body mapped like a continent — each scar a river, each bruise a mountain range, each stretch mark a road, each freckle a city. It is an inhabited landscape.

ideas.title: Mushroom Lanterns at Dusk
ideas.description: Giant mushrooms glowing softly in a dark forest at dusk, their undersides lit from within, casting warm pink and amber light on the moss below. Fireflies circle them.

ideas.title: The Invisible Orchestra
ideas.description: An empty stage with chairs and music stands, but the instruments float in midair, playing themselves. The music is visible — colored sound waves rippling outward.

ideas.title: Cloud Person's Afternoon
ideas.description: A being made entirely of cloud sitting on a park bench, reading a newspaper. Pigeons perch on their shoulder. They leave a small damp patch on the bench when they rise to leave.

ideas.title: The Tea Ceremony of Storms
ideas.description: Two storm clouds sitting across from each other at a low wooden table, performing a tea ceremony with lightning poured carefully from a ceramic pot into tiny thunder-cups.

ideas.title: Snow Globe City
ideas.description: A massive snow globe containing an entire living city — the people inside don't know they're inside one. A giant hand is pressed against the glass, peering in.

ideas.title: The Seed Librarian
ideas.description: An old botanist who keeps seeds in a library — each tiny seed filed alphabetically in paper envelopes, their potential futures annotated in pencil in the margins.

ideas.title: Painting That Rains Inside
ideas.description: A framed oil painting on a museum wall — but inside the canvas it is raining, and tiny puddles are forming at the bottom of the frame, spilling out onto the floor.

ideas.title: Cartographer of Forgotten Places
ideas.description: A woman drawing maps of places that only exist in dreams — cities dreamed once and never returned to. The maps curl at the edges and smell faintly of sleep.

ideas.title: Moth That Eats Darkness
ideas.description: A large pale moth that feeds on patches of darkness — it lands on shadows and slowly dissolves them, leaving faint glowing traces on the ground where darkness used to be.

ideas.title: The Colour Thief's Workshop
ideas.description: A small room where someone extracts colour from objects — squeezing the blue from a sky in jars, pressing the green from leaves into vials. The stolen things remain grey.

ideas.title: The Bridge Made of Birdsong
ideas.description: A bridge that becomes solid only when birds sing — each note adds a plank of golden light. In silence it vanishes. People must carry songbirds in their pockets to cross.

ideas.title: Jar of Collected Mornings
ideas.description: A mason jar filled with the light of various mornings — each layer a different shade of dawn, rose, amber, pale gold — preserved like jam, to be spread on bread when the day is grey.

ideas.title: The Glacier That Remembers
ideas.description: An ancient glacier with memories frozen inside it — faces, sounds, whole landscapes visible through the blue ice. Scientists press their ears to it to hear the past.

ideas.title: Sparrow Post Office
ideas.description: A tiny post office run by sparrows — each letter is tied to a leg, and the counter is a twig, and the sorting room is a hollow oak. The postmaster wears a cap made of an acorn.

ideas.title: Rain Portrait
ideas.description: A figure standing in the rain whose face is formed only by the way raindrops fall around it — the rain avoids certain spaces, creating eyes, a nose, a slight smile.

ideas.title: The Terrarium Planet
ideas.description: A glass terrarium containing a tiny self-sufficient planet — its own weather system, miniature mountain ranges, a thumb-sized ocean with waves. A child observes it with a magnifying glass.

ideas.title: Grandmother's Recipe for Thunder
ideas.description: A worn recipe card in a kitchen drawer titled 'Thursday Thunder.' Ingredients: one bruised sky, a cup of humidity, patience, and the distant smell of rain. Method: wait.

ideas.title: The Tide That Carries Wishes
ideas.description: At a particular beach, when the tide goes out it takes something from each person's mind — a wish, a worry, a secret hope. The ocean sorts them and returns only the wishes.

ideas.title: Velvet Cave of Echoes
ideas.description: A cave whose walls are lined with dark velvet that absorbs all sound — except whispers, which it catches and replays back as songs hours later, in different voices.

ideas.title: The Sailor of Rooftops
ideas.description: An old sailor who retired from the sea and now navigates rooftops by starlight — rigging clotheslines as sails, reading chimney smoke like wind direction, mapping the city like an ocean.

ideas.title: Moon Moth Migration
ideas.description: Thousands of luminescent moon moths migrating across a night sky, their pale wings casting silver light below them, turning dark fields into something that looks like moonlit water.

ideas.title: The Painter's Last Blue
ideas.description: An elderly painter has run out of all colors except one final tube of blue. She paints everything blue — the sky, the sea, her cat, her own hands — and finds it is enough.

ideas.title: House Built from Old Arguments
ideas.description: A crooked house whose bricks are made of old arguments — some bricks hot to the touch, some cold, some crumbling. The windows are made of resolved misunderstandings, perfectly clear.

ideas.title: The Clockwork Forest
ideas.description: A forest where each tree is mechanical — gears visible beneath bark, leaves that tick as they turn, roots that wind like clock springs underground, all keeping perfect time.

ideas.title: Keeper of Lost Afternoons
ideas.description: A small museum where lost afternoons are kept — each displayed in a glass case with a label: Tuesday, 3pm, the smell of cut grass, a forgotten name. Visitors weep gently.

ideas.title: Desert Rain in Slow Motion
ideas.description: A single rainstorm over a desert, each drop falling in slow motion — so slow you can watch a wildflower bloom from seed to flower as one drop descends toward it.

ideas.title: The Night Market of Lost Things
ideas.description: A market that appears only after midnight selling things people have lost — not just objects, but lost words, lost songs half-remembered, lost names, lost versions of yourself.

ideas.title: The Hummingbird Clockmaker
ideas.description: A hummingbird who repairs the tiny clocks inside flowers — each bloom has a miniature mechanism that controls when it opens. The hummingbird carries a jeweller's loupe and tweezers.

ideas.title: Cathedral of Ice
ideas.description: A cathedral carved entirely from glacier ice, deep in an arctic landscape. Stained glass windows made of colored ice throw fractured rainbow light across the frozen pews.

ideas.title: The Boy Who Kept the Sun in His Pocket
ideas.description: A young boy with a small sun kept in his coat pocket — it peeks out as warm golden light. On cloudy days he lets it out a little more. On sunny days he lets others borrow it.

ideas.title: Fog Whisperer's Garden
ideas.description: A garden tended by someone who whispers to the fog, coaxing it into shapes — fog roses that open slowly, fog animals that drink from fog ponds, fog children who play until noon.

ideas.title: The Compass That Points to Feeling
ideas.description: An old brass compass whose needle doesn't point north — it points toward whatever you most need. Sometimes it points behind you. Sometimes it spins wildly and points inward.

ideas.title: Lantern Festival on a Flooded Street
ideas.description: A street flooded ankle-deep with still water, lit lanterns floating on the surface, their reflections perfect doubles below. The whole city watches from windows.

ideas.title: The Dream Taxidermist
ideas.description: A taxidermist who preserves dreams instead of animals — pinning the wings of night terrors, mounting the forms of beautiful recurring dreams, labeling them with small brass tags.

ideas.title: Cloud Reading Lesson
ideas.description: A classroom in the sky — children sitting on clouds as desks, a teacher pointing to cumulus formations with a long wand, a chalkboard that is actually sky, chalk marks in white cloud.

ideas.title: The River Made of Hours
ideas.description: A river that flows not with water but with hours — you can see 3pm passing, Tuesday morning, a late October afternoon. People wade in and sometimes lose an afternoon to the current.

ideas.title: The Astronomer of Small Things
ideas.description: A scientist who studies the universe within small things — the spiral galaxy inside a snail shell, the nebula in a drop of ink, the black hole at the center of a grape.

ideas.title: Rain Collector's Museum
ideas.description: A museum of rain samples from around the world — monsoon rain from Kerala, desert rain from Namibia, rain from the day of a historic event. Each in a sealed jar with audio of the storm.

ideas.title: The Woman Who Swallowed a Star
ideas.description: A woman who once swallowed a star by accident — it lives in her chest now, glowing faintly through her ribs, warming everyone near her. She can't see it but others always can.

ideas.title: Bookshelf of Unread Possibilities
ideas.description: A towering bookshelf full of books whose spines show paths not taken — 'If You Had Stayed,' 'The Version of You That Moved to Rome,' 'What Happens if You Say Yes.' All unread.

ideas.title: The Map of Every Kindness
ideas.description: A map of a city where every act of kindness ever performed is marked — the city is so densely dotted with gold stars it glows from above. Some streets are solid gold.

ideas.title: Antique Shop of Borrowed Feelings
ideas.description: A dusty antique shop where you can buy the feeling of someone else's first love, or their particular shade of homesickness. Each bottled feeling is priced by how rare it is.

ideas.title: Owl Who Delivers Sunrises
ideas.description: A great grey owl who carries the sunrise in a satchel, delivering it to different corners of the world each morning. She has a detailed route map and is never late.

ideas.title: The Skeleton Library
ideas.description: A library where every book is written in bone — the pages are thin wafers of ivory, the words carved in miniature. The oldest books are read only with magnifying lenses.

ideas.title: Lavender Farm on the Ceiling
ideas.description: Lavender growing downward from a ceiling, roots in the plaster, flowers dangling like purple chandeliers. Bees navigate upside down. The room always smells of summer.

ideas.title: The Giant's Thimble
ideas.description: A thimble the size of a house, half-buried in a meadow, used by an enormous seamstress who stitches the horizon together each dawn. Thread the color of sunrise trails behind the hills.

ideas.title: The Expedition into Childhood
ideas.description: Scientists in expedition gear carefully entering a childhood bedroom — taking soil samples from the carpet, studying the fossils of lego pieces, photographing the faded wallpaper with reverence.

ideas.title: Portrait of a Sunday Afternoon
ideas.description: A painted portrait not of a person but of a feeling — the exact quality of light on a Sunday at 4pm, the particular weight of silence, the warm amber of a nearly finished week.

ideas.title: The Cartographer's Ghost
ideas.description: The ghost of a long-dead cartographer still roaming their old workshop, unable to rest because the map of the afterlife is incomplete. They keep adding new territories every century.

ideas.title: Architecture of a Storm
ideas.description: A cross-section diagram of a thunderstorm treated as architecture — labeled rooms (the Anger Chamber, the Grief Hall, the Quiet Eye), structural columns of lightning, stairwells of updraft.

ideas.title: The Keeper of Waiting Rooms
ideas.description: An old woman who tends to all the waiting rooms in the world — she keeps the plants alive, replaces the out-of-date magazines, dims the light to something bearable, and hums softly.

ideas.title: The Archive of Last Sounds
ideas.description: A room full of jars containing the last sounds of extinct things — the last call of the passenger pigeon, the last creak of a demolished building, the last laugh of someone long gone.

ideas.title: Cartographer's Birthday Party
ideas.description: A birthday party where, instead of balloons, the guests release handmade maps into the sky — each one charting a place they love. The sky fills with parchment and longitude lines.

ideas.title: The Inkwell Ocean
ideas.description: A vast ocean made entirely of ink — black and deep, with waves that leave dark stains on the white sand shore. Creatures made of unwritten words drift beneath the surface.

ideas.title: The Dew Collector's Morning
ideas.description: Before dawn, a figure moves through a meadow with silk cloths, collecting dew — each drop a different flavour depending on which flower it rested on. Bottled as rare medicine.

ideas.title: Stars Asleep in a Drawer
ideas.description: An old bureau drawer pulled open to reveal a cluster of small sleeping stars — curled up like cats, humming faintly, barely glowing. The drawer is lined with velvet the color of midnight.

ideas.title: The Geographer of Interiors
ideas.description: A scientist who maps the interior spaces of houses — the emotional weather of living rooms, the altitude of attics, the hidden caves behind wallpaper. Produces maps of feeling.

ideas.title: Festival of Falling Leaves
ideas.description: An annual autumn festival where the trees are the performers — each one dropping leaves in deliberate patterns, choreographed like dancers. The audience sits in the roots.

ideas.title: The Grief Taxidermist
ideas.description: Someone who preserves grief into beautiful objects — a hollow sphere of amber containing the shape of a goodbye, a glass bird full of the particular silence after loss.

ideas.title: The Underground Sky
ideas.description: A vast underground cavern where the ceiling is covered in bioluminescent organisms that pulse like stars — entire underground civilisations navigate by this false sky.

ideas.title: Painter of the In-Between
ideas.description: An artist who paints only transitional moments — the exact second between sleeping and waking, the space between one breath and the next, the moment before a decision.

ideas.title: The Apiary of Stolen Hours
ideas.description: A hive where bees collect not pollen but stolen hours — time stolen from busy people who don't notice a Tuesday morning vanish. The honey is sweet and makes you dream of slow days.

ideas.title: The Shore Where the Sea Whispers Names
ideas.description: A shore where, if you listen closely, the waves whisper names — names of people who have loved the sea, names of ships, names the ocean invented for things without words.

ideas.title: The Repair Shop for Broken Mornings
ideas.description: A small shop that takes in broken mornings — mornings that started wrong, spilled coffee, missed alarms — and repairs them. Returns them as small glowing objects you can revisit.

ideas.title: The Cloud Stitcher
ideas.description: A figure high above the earth stitching clouds together with enormous needles and thread made of sunlight — reinforcing them so they can hold heavy rain without tearing apart.

ideas.title: The Ant Cartographer
ideas.description: A single ant mapping an entire garden from the ground level — mountains are pebbles, oceans are puddles, forests are grass blades. The map is drawn on a grain of rice.

ideas.title: Abandoned Submarine Garden
ideas.description: An old submarine resting on the ocean floor, its hatches open, coral growing through the periscope, a garden of anemones in the torpedo tubes, fish darting through the control room.

ideas.title: The Thunderstorm Barber
ideas.description: A barber shop where the barber is a storm cloud — lightning scissors that cut precisely, rain that washes, wind that styles. Thunder rolls approvingly when a good cut is finished.

ideas.title: The Painter's Sunrise Ritual
ideas.description: Every morning a painter wakes before dawn to watch the sunrise, mixing the exact colors she sees in real time, as if painting the sky would make it stay. The colors never fully match.

ideas.title: River of Forgotten Songs
ideas.description: A river that carries fragments of half-remembered songs — you can hear snatches of melody if you press your ear to the water. Children come to fish out tunes with their fingers.

ideas.title: The Garden of Careful Mistakes
ideas.description: A garden where beautiful plants grow only from mistakes — the flower that blooms from a wrong turn, the tree that grew from a miscalculation. Each labeled with what went wrong.

ideas.title: Snow That Falls Upward
ideas.description: In a particular valley, snow falls upward — from the ground into the sky, gathering into clouds above. Children lie in the grass and watch it rise, catching flakes on their noses.

ideas.title: The Moth's Library
ideas.description: A library built entirely for moths — the books are made of silk and the words are patterns of light. Moths read with their wings open, drinking in the illuminated pages.

ideas.title: The Shadow's Holiday
ideas.description: A shadow that has taken a holiday from its person — traveling alone, visiting bright sunny places, eating ice cream that melts in the heat. The person walks slightly flattened without it.

ideas.title: The Archaeologist of Today
ideas.description: An archaeologist who excavates the present — carefully brushing away seconds to examine minutes, cataloguing the afternoon, publishing a report on This Tuesday specifically.

ideas.title: The Bridge Between Seasons
ideas.description: A wooden bridge that spans the exact moment between summer and autumn — one end sun-warm, the other in amber leaf-fall. Standing in the middle you can feel both at once.

ideas.title: Fossil of a Wednesday
ideas.description: A geological cross-section revealing a fossilized Wednesday — the layers show morning coffee, a lunch break, an afternoon slump, the commute home. Scientists call it unremarkable. It is perfect.

ideas.title: The Seamstress of Seasons
ideas.description: A seamstress who sews the seasons together — giant panels of green summer, gold autumn, white winter, pink spring — basting them with invisible thread to make one unbroken year.

ideas.title: House of Unfinished Sentences
ideas.description: A house where all the walls are covered in unfinished sentences — painted on plaster, carved in wood, scrawled in chalk. The residents live inside everyone's trailing thoughts.

ideas.title: The Understudy for the Sun
ideas.description: On days the sun calls in sick, its understudy arrives — a slightly smaller, pinkish star that tries its best but forgets to set until 10pm and makes the shadows fall the wrong way.

ideas.title: The Watcher of Doorways
ideas.description: An ancient spirit who lives in doorways, observing all the entrances and exits, keeping tally of who enters nervously and who leaves with relief. Old doors remember everything.

ideas.title: The Painter of First Meetings
ideas.description: An artist commissioned only to paint the exact moment two people first meet — the specific quality of awkward warmth, the unplanned turning toward each other, a background of ordinary life.

ideas.title: The Architect of Clouds
ideas.description: Someone who designs clouds from a studio high in the atmosphere — sketching new formations, deciding which ones get lightning, signing their best work in cirrus before letting them go.

ideas.title: The Amber Room of Ordinary Moments
ideas.description: A room where every ordinary moment has been preserved in amber — a sneeze, an eye-rub, a moment of staring out a bus window — each specimen labeled with the exact time and date.

ideas.title: Moonrise Swimming Pool
ideas.description: A rooftop swimming pool where, on certain nights, the reflection of the moon fills the entire pool with silver light. Swimmers float in what looks like pure moonlight.

ideas.title: The Tailor of Clouds
ideas.description: A tailor who cuts and sews clouds into garments for giants — a cirrus scarf, a cumulus overcoat, a thin stratus dress for summer. The giants wear them during mountain storms.

ideas.title: The Paleontologist of Feelings
ideas.description: A scientist who excavates ancient feelings from geological strata — carefully extracting a 2,000-year-old longing, a prehistoric joy still perfectly intact, a grief fossilized mid-exhale.

ideas.title: Night Train to Nowhere in Particular
ideas.description: A train that runs only at night with no fixed destination — passengers climb on sleepy, the landscape changes outside as they doze, and they arrive somewhere they needed to be.

ideas.title: The Botanist Who Grows Feelings
ideas.description: A botanist growing emotional plants — a climbing vine of longing, a succulent holding a week's worth of contentment in each leaf, a drooping fern that is always slightly melancholy.

ideas.title: The Shepherd of Shooting Stars
ideas.description: A shepherd who tends to shooting stars like a flock — calling them back when they stray, patching their tails when they fray, releasing them on schedule over different continents.

ideas.title: The Island That Follows Ships
ideas.description: An island that quietly follows ships it likes — keeping a respectful distance, appearing in the background of sunsets, gone by morning but visible again three days later.

ideas.title: Portrait of a Person Who Has Never Slept
ideas.description: A portrait of someone who hasn't slept in years — their eyes hold entire extra worlds in them, dark circles like satellite photos of storm systems, eyebrows perpetually raised in exhausted wonder.

ideas.title: The Season No One Named
ideas.description: A fifth season that lasts only a few days in some years — the air is warm and cold at once, the light is amber and violet simultaneously, and everyone acts slightly more honest than usual.

ideas.title: Tea Made from Rainbows
ideas.description: A tea shop that serves teas brewed from different parts of rainbows — red is warming and bold, violet is calming and slightly sleepy, green has the taste of a good decision made early.

ideas.title: The Abandoned Carnival of Dreams
ideas.description: A carnival that no longer operates during waking hours — rides only turn at 3am, the ferris wheel lit by fireflies, the music box plays a song no one recognizes but everyone knows.

ideas.title: The Coral Philosopher
ideas.description: A coral reef colony that thinks collectively — each polyp a neuron in a vast slow mind, contemplating the ocean across geological time, arriving at conclusions over centuries.

ideas.title: The Lighthouse Keeper's Diary
ideas.description: A lighthouse keeper who writes daily diaries of what the light sees — ships named by feeling, storms given personal history, fog described as a visitor with no business being here.

ideas.title: The Observatory of Ordinary Stars
ideas.description: An observatory dedicated only to unremarkable, average stars — dim ones, plain ones, stars no one has named. The astronomer argues they are the backbone of the universe.

ideas.title: The Forest That Sings at Noon
ideas.description: A forest where, precisely at noon, all the trees exhale simultaneously and produce a low harmonic chord that lasts exactly two minutes. Animals stop and listen. So do people.

ideas.title: Ceramic Moon Repairs
ideas.description: A workshop where craftspeople repair damage to the moon each month — filling craters with pale ceramic glaze, buffing the surface to a shine, sending it back before the next full moon.

ideas.title: The Migration of Umbrellas
ideas.description: Every autumn, lost umbrellas migrate south in enormous flocks — filling the sky with their black canopies, moving in formation. No one knows where they go or why they return wet.

ideas.title: The Colour of Silence
ideas.description: A painting attempting to capture the exact colour of silence — a shade slightly off-white, with traces of pale blue, that shifts depending on the silence around it when viewed.

ideas.title: Architect of Morning
ideas.description: Someone who builds mornings from scratch each day — assembling light from the east, warming the air in layers, placing birdsong in sequence, releasing the smell of damp earth on schedule.

ideas.title: The Velvet Astronomer
ideas.description: An astronomer who insists on velvet for everything — velvet telescope covers, velvet sky charts, velvet gloves to handle star maps. Claims fabric softens the harshness of infinity.

ideas.title: Frozen Moment at a Train Station
ideas.description: Everything at a busy train station frozen in time — departing passengers mid-stride, pigeons mid-flap, steam mid-billow — except one small child still moving slowly through the stillness.

ideas.title: The City Built on Fog
ideas.description: A coastal city whose foundations rest entirely on a bed of dense fog — the buildings don't sink because no one has tried to prove they should. It works on belief.

ideas.title: The Librarian Who Remembers Every Book
ideas.description: An ancient librarian who has read every book ever written — her mind is a perfect library. When books are lost to fire, she sits quietly and reproduces them from memory.

ideas.title: The Ballroom of Parallel Lives
ideas.description: A ballroom where alternate versions of yourself dance — you as you would have been if you'd moved away, if you'd taken the other job, if you'd been braver about one particular thing.

ideas.title: The Cloud Shepherd's Winter
ideas.description: A cloud shepherd in deep winter, when the clouds are heavy and grey and reluctant — coaxing them gently, keeping the flock together in blizzards, nursing sick ones back to white.

ideas.title: Sunrise Fishing for Stars
ideas.description: At dawn, a fisherman casts his line into the fading sky and reels in the last remaining stars before daylight swallows them. He keeps the smallest ones in a tank at home.

ideas.title: Portrait of a Season Changing Its Mind
ideas.description: Autumn halfway through deciding to become winter — one half of the scene in golden leaves and amber warmth, the other half gone white and still, divided by a single bare tree.

ideas.title: The Bird That Collects Regrets
ideas.description: A large silent bird that visits people in the night and takes one regret from their chest — leaving a small smooth stone in its place. The bird's feathers are made of accumulated whispers.

ideas.title: The Sundial at the End of Autumn
ideas.description: An ancient sundial in a forest clearing, still keeping perfect time though the trees are bare and the light comes slant and thin. Leaves collect in its hour markers like gifts.

ideas.title: The Museum of Unlived Days
ideas.description: A museum displaying days you never had — days spent in places you never visited, in versions of yourself you never became. Each exhibit is a perfect Tuesday someone else lived.

ideas.title: The Sky Tailor
ideas.description: A figure who tailors the sky to fit each day — letting it out on vast cloudless days, taking it in on grey close days, adding embellishments of cirrus for special occasions.

ideas.title: Whale Songs in the Mountains
ideas.description: Mountain ranges that emit whale songs during strong winds — the peaks and valleys shaped exactly like cetacean vocal anatomy. Climbers hear deep calls from inside the rock.

ideas.title: The Florist of Extinct Flowers
ideas.description: A florist who grows flowers from seeds archived from extinct species — species of blossom not seen for centuries, colors that exist nowhere else, perfumes the world forgot it missed.

ideas.title: The Person Who Collects Twilights
ideas.description: Someone who photographs every twilight they've ever witnessed — not the sunset itself, but the exact ten minutes after, when the sky goes from rose to deepest blue. An archive of transitions.

ideas.title: The Cave of All Echoes
ideas.description: A cave system where every sound ever made inside still reverberates — you can hear a child's laughter from 200 years ago, a monk's prayer, a woman humming while lost.

ideas.title: The Festival of Returning Things
ideas.description: An annual festival where lost items return on their own — socks, single earrings, library books 30 years overdue, a word you couldn't remember, a feeling you thought was gone for good.

ideas.title: The Garden at the End of the World
ideas.description: A small, well-tended garden right at the edge where the world ends — the gardener plants bulbs for flowers that will bloom into the void, seeds the abyss with marigolds.

ideas.title: Ice Sculptor of Rain
ideas.description: An ice sculptor who freezes rain mid-fall and carves it into figures — each rainstorm providing new material, each sculpture temporary, melting into the next storm.

ideas.title: The Ship Made of Fog
ideas.description: A sailing ship made entirely of dense fog — rigging, hull and sails all grey mist. It can sail anywhere because it cannot be stopped by rocks it simply passes through.

ideas.title: The Tender of Old Fires
ideas.description: Someone whose job is to tend fires that have never been allowed to go out — ancient flame traditions spanning millennia. The oldest fire in their keeping is older than the language to name it.

ideas.title: Portrait of the Smell of Rain
ideas.description: A sensory illustration attempting to capture petrichor — the scene before rain, dry earth, the moment the first drop hits stone, the particular exhalation of a relieved landscape.

ideas.title: The Star That Got Lost
ideas.description: A single star that wandered from its constellation and is now alone in a patch of sky, winking anxiously, sending light signals in all directions hoping to be recognized and welcomed back.

ideas.title: The Kingdom Under the Stairs
ideas.description: The tiny kingdom living under a staircase — ruled by a beetle in a thimble crown, with kingdoms of dust and lint, trade routes along the baseboard, and wars over crumbs.

ideas.title: The Sleeping Forest
ideas.description: An entire forest that hibernates each winter — not just animals but the trees themselves, their sap slowed to dreaming, roots pulling warmth inward, every leaf memory tucked away.

ideas.title: The Musician Who Plays Weather
ideas.description: A musician whose instrument is the landscape itself — plucking cables for wind, drumming rooftops for rain, drawing a bow across a power line for the long note of a grey afternoon.

ideas.title: Garden Growing in a Teacup
ideas.description: An entire wild garden growing inside a porcelain teacup — tiny oak tree, meadow of thyme, a pond for a raindrop, bees smaller than punctuation marks tending to everything.

ideas.title: The Ghost of a Building That's Not Yet Built
ideas.description: The ghost of a building that will be constructed in the future — already haunting the empty lot, its transparent floors and windows visible at night, footsteps heard before the foundation is laid.

ideas.title: Library in a Lighthouse
ideas.description: A lighthouse whose light chamber has been turned into a library — books arranged in a spiral following the beam, reading in the slow sweep of light, the sea visible through every gap.

ideas.title: The Afternoon That Never Left
ideas.description: One golden summer afternoon that got stuck — it never turned to evening. The same 4pm light falls perpetually, the shadows don't move, and the people who live there have forgotten it should change.

ideas.title: Rain That Falls Sideways on Purpose
ideas.description: Rain that deliberately falls sideways, adjusting its trajectory to avoid hitting people it likes. Those who pay attention notice the rain parting gently around them.

ideas.title: The Owl Post Between Worlds
ideas.description: A postal system run by owls that carries mail between this world and adjacent ones — the letters arrive damp, smelling faintly of somewhere almost-familiar. Replies take seven years.

ideas.title: Botanical Survey of a Dream
ideas.description: A scientific field report on the vegetation encountered in a recurring dream — species list, sketches, notes on soil composition. The plants have no equivalent in waking botany.

ideas.title: The Storm That Fell in Love with a Garden
ideas.description: A violent thunderstorm that arrived to destroy a garden but fell in love with it instead — now it circles overhead protectively, giving only gentle rain, frightening away hailstorms.

ideas.title: The Ice Hotel of Extinct Languages
ideas.description: An ice hotel where every room is carved in a different extinct language — walls, ceilings and floors inscribed with words no one has spoken in centuries. The cold preserves them perfectly.

ideas.title: The Maker of Tiny Worlds
ideas.description: A craftsperson who builds entire worlds in glass bottles — each one fully functioning, with tiny weather patterns, miniature civilizations, their own small histories playing out.

ideas.title: The Person Who Is Always Arriving
ideas.description: A figure perpetually mid-arrival — always just stepping off a train, just pushing open a door, always bringing the outside in on their coat. Never quite settled. Always just here.

ideas.title: The Mountain That Hums
ideas.description: A dormant mountain that produces a low harmonic hum from its volcanic chambers — the local people use it to tune their instruments, orientate themselves in fog, and fall asleep.

ideas.title: The Painter Who Paints Closed Eyes
ideas.description: An artist who paints only what people see behind closed eyelids — the private colours, patterns and half-formed images. The gallery is experienced only with eyes shut.

ideas.title: The River at the End of Memory
ideas.description: A river that flows at the edge of memory — things you can almost remember drift on its surface, half-visible. If you don't reach for them in time, the current takes them away.

ideas.title: The Garden in the Storm's Eye
ideas.description: A perfectly calm garden growing inside the eye of a hurricane — topiary, a birdbath still as glass, bees unhurried. The walls of the storm visible all around, but here: stillness.

ideas.title: Cartography of a Friendship
ideas.description: A hand-drawn map of a long friendship — mountains of misunderstanding, rivers of laughter, deserts of not speaking for months, the capital city of a single ordinary evening.

ideas.title: The Candle that Burns Backwards
ideas.description: A candle that, instead of melting down, grows taller as it burns — and the flame at the top is cold. As days pass the candle extends toward the ceiling, cool and white.

ideas.title: The Expedition to the Centre of a Raindrop
ideas.description: A miniature expedition inside a single raindrop — scientists in diving gear exploring the curved interior, charting the tiny world refracted there, discovering it holds a whole landscape upside down.

ideas.title: The Winged Library
ideas.description: A library that migrates annually like a bird — lifting off on enormous soft wings, following warmth, settling in a new location each year. Borrowers find it by following birdsong.

ideas.title: The Last Train Into Autumn
ideas.description: A train that runs only once a year, on the first day of autumn — carrying the summer away. Passengers feel warm all through autumn because they were on that train once.

ideas.title: The Snow That Speaks
ideas.description: Snow that falls in patterns that, if read correctly, form sentences — meteorologists trained in this language translate them. Yesterday's blizzard was a long complaint about how warm it's been.

ideas.title: The Botanist's Ghost
ideas.description: The ghost of a Victorian botanist still wandering their overgrown greenhouse — still making notes in a spectral journal, pressing ghost flowers, cataloguing plants that have long since died.

ideas.title: The Tide That Speaks at Low Water
ideas.description: A tidal beach where, at lowest tide, rocks inscribed with messages from the deep are exposed — geological braille, old notation, a tide chart written by the ocean for itself.

ideas.title: The Fog That Writes Letters
ideas.description: A particular fog that settles each autumn morning and leaves letters on windows — condensation shaped into words. The letters are in no language anyone recognizes, but they feel important.

ideas.title: The Crow Who Writes Elegies
ideas.description: A crow perched on a fence post, writing small elegies for things that have disappeared — the dead telephone boxes, the extinct flavors of old candy, the particular shade of a childhood bedroom.

ideas.title: The Deep Sea Cafe
ideas.description: A cafe at the bottom of the Mariana Trench — pressure-resistant walls of deep blue glass, anglerfish serving as lamps, a menu written in bioluminescence. The coffee is somehow perfect.

ideas.title: The Architect Who Builds in Fog
ideas.description: An architect who has developed a technique for building permanent structures from fog — fog bridges, fog towers, fog cathedrals. They stand. Scientists refuse to explain why.

ideas.title: The Person Who Collects Silences
ideas.description: A collector with hundreds of jars labeled: 'Library silence, 3pm Tuesday,' 'The silence after news,' 'Forest silence — no wind.' The most precious jar holds 'The silence before music begins.'

ideas.title: The Island That Breathes
ideas.description: An island whose coastline expands and contracts rhythmically — the beach grows at dawn, shrinks at dusk, as if the island is breathing. The rocks are its ribs.

ideas.title: Field Study of a Rainy Day
ideas.description: A scientific field notebook documenting a single rainy day — hourly observations of the rain's mood, specimen collection of notable puddles, a taxonomy of umbrellas seen.

ideas.title: The Braid of Rivers
ideas.description: Three rivers that braid together in a braided delta — when viewed from above, they plait and unplait continuously like hair, some channels joining for miles before separating again.

ideas.title: The Moth and the Streetlamp
ideas.description: A moth in love with a particular streetlamp — it knows the lamp's schedule, waits patiently through daylight, vibrates with joy at dusk. The lamp, for its part, shines a little warmer.

ideas.title: The Polar Bear's Library
ideas.description: A library buried in arctic ice, maintained by a single polar bear who reads by bioluminescence, shelves books by weight, and has written extensive marginalia on every page in fish oil ink.

ideas.title: Portrait of an Argument That Became Music
ideas.description: A painting of the moment an argument between two people becomes something else — their voices rising and falling into involuntary harmony, the room going still, both of them surprised.

ideas.title: The Philosopher's Garden
ideas.description: An overgrown garden where a philosopher has labeled everything with its philosophical implication — 'This rose: impermanence,' 'This pebble: the problem of weight,' 'This shadow: doubt.'

ideas.title: The Wax Museum of Emotions
ideas.description: A wax museum where the exhibits are perfectly recreated human emotions — Contentment at 4pm on a Sunday, Surprising Grief while Doing Dishes, Unexpected Joy at an Airport.

ideas.title: The Child Who Understood Weather
ideas.description: A child who can speak to weather systems — negotiating with approaching storms, soothing anxious low-pressure systems, convincing fog to lift before school. Weather mostly cooperates.

ideas.title: The Ghost of an Unfinished Symphony
ideas.description: A symphony that was never completed haunting a concert hall — the orchestra hears the missing movements during silence, the ghost notes filling in what was never written.

ideas.title: The Shore at the Edge of Sleep
ideas.description: A coastline that exists only in the seconds between waking and sleep — a warm beach with familiar-strange dunes, water that is also sky, a lighthouse that blinks in morse for your name.

ideas.title: The Two Moons
ideas.description: A sky with two moons — one the real moon, the other its reflection caught in a high altitude cloud and refused to come down. The reflected moon is slightly warmer in color.

ideas.title: The Fisherman of Lost Thoughts
ideas.description: An old man fishing in a mental river — his line goes into memory itself, and he reels up half-thoughts, forgotten names, the fragment of a melody. He keeps the useful ones in a bucket.

ideas.title: The Cartographer of Scent
ideas.description: A scientist who maps invisible territories of smell — the territory of bread from the bakery, the cinnamon kingdom, the borderlands where rain meets dry stone. Maps sold to the nostalgia-prone.

ideas.title: Knitting Club at the End of the Universe
ideas.description: A small knitting club meeting at the very edge of the universe — five women quietly knitting, the void immediately outside the window, tea growing cold, someone's pattern going wrong.

ideas.title: The Map of Every Goodbye
ideas.description: A city map with every place a goodbye was said marked — a red dot outside this station, a small circle at this door, a long line where someone walked away slowly. Dense in certain streets.

ideas.title: The Library at the Centre of the Storm
ideas.description: A library in perfect stillness at the eye of a hurricane — bookshelves undisturbed, pages unruffled, a librarian shelving books calmly while the storm rotates around them.

ideas.title: Migration of Ideas
ideas.description: Ideas visualized as physical creatures migrating across the sky — half-formed thoughts trailing behind fully realized ones, discarded drafts forming a slow tail, the great flock of breakthrough concepts.

ideas.title: The Shore of Almost
ideas.description: A shoreline made of the things that almost happened — the conversation you almost started, the door you almost opened, the word you almost said. They wash up here, small and smooth.

ideas.title: The Polar Expedition Café
ideas.description: An expedition-style café in an arctic setting — tent canvas walls, equipment as furniture, the warmest coffee imaginable, the sound of wind outside, a map covered in frostbitten fingerprints.

ideas.title: Portrait of Someone Looking at the Stars
ideas.description: A back-view portrait of a figure lying in grass looking up at the sky — the stars visible both in the sky above and in their eyes below, as if the sky goes all the way through them.

ideas.title: The Glassblower of Clouds
ideas.description: A glassblower who produces clouds by blowing into a long glass tube aimed at the sky — the breath forms cumulus, stratus, cumulonimbus depending on technique. Weather reports credit them.

ideas.title: The Museum of Ordinary Tuesdays
ideas.description: A museum dedicated entirely to the ordinary Tuesday — each gallery a different unremarkable Tuesday from history, perfectly recreated. The most visited exhibit: a Tuesday with nothing happening at all.

ideas.title: The Last Firefly
ideas.description: The last firefly in a meadow, blinking alone at dusk — but it blinks on and off with such conviction, such steadiness, that the darkness around it becomes its own kind of answer.

ideas.title: The Garden Party for Old Trees
ideas.description: A formal garden party held by very old trees — the invitations are carved into bark and delivered by beetles, the refreshments are rainfall and sunlight, and the conversation spans centuries.

ideas.title: The Clocktower That Keeps Dream Time
ideas.description: A clocktower that runs on dream time — it moves fast through boring stretches, slows for moments of wonder, stops entirely during the best dreams. No one sets their watch by it but everyone checks it.

ideas.title: The Philosopher's Rock
ideas.description: A boulder that has spent 10,000 years thinking. It has arrived at several conclusions. They are written in the frost on its surface each winter morning and melt by noon.

ideas.title: The Archive of Abandoned Nests
ideas.description: A naturalist's archive of abandoned bird nests — each documented, photographed, the materials listed. The archive reads like a record of small abandonings, each one a different story.

ideas.title: The Horizon That Moves
ideas.description: A landscape painting whose horizon moves — not dramatically, just slightly, between viewings. It has been moving for 400 years. It is almost at the top of the canvas now.

ideas.title: The Weathervane That Tells Feelings
ideas.description: An old weathervane that stopped pointing to wind direction and now points toward where the nearest person is feeling most intensely. In cities it spins without stopping.

ideas.title: The Baker of Stars
ideas.description: A baker who bakes stars in a wood-fired oven — kneading the luminous dough before dawn, shaping each one carefully, tapping when they sound hollow, sliding the finished stars into the sky.

ideas.title: The Quiet Hours Preserved in Glass
ideas.description: A collection of glass spheres, each containing a preserved quiet hour — a 2am Tuesday, a snowy Sunday morning, a rainy April Thursday at 6pm. They glow faintly in the dark.

ideas.title: The Whale Who Swallowed a Library
ideas.description: A blue whale who once accidentally swallowed a sunken library — the books slowly dissolve over decades and the whale sings stories now, not songs. People follow its migration to listen.

ideas.title: The Garden of Good Mornings
ideas.description: A garden where every plant represents a different kind of good morning — coffee-and-sun mornings, slow foggy mornings with toast, unexpected joy before breakfast mornings. Each labeled with care.

ideas.title: The Roof of the World's Last Cloud
ideas.description: A future world where only one cloud remains — preserved in a climate facility, curators who remember clouds, schoolchildren visiting on trips to see the last remaining cumulus.

ideas.title: The Navigator of Dreams
ideas.description: An old sailor who navigates through other people's sleeping minds at night — charting their dream geography, marking dangerous waters, leaving small lighthouse-flames at the frightening parts.

ideas.title: The Clockwork Hummingbird
ideas.description: A mechanical hummingbird with gears for a heart, wings of hammered copper, eyes of deep amber glass — built by a clockmaker who missed his garden and all its real ones.

ideas.title: The Girl Who Grew Moons
ideas.description: A girl with a rooftop garden where she grows moons from seed — small ones like fruit, large ones like pumpkins. The harvest happens in autumn and the sky gets restocked for winter.

ideas.title: The Clockwork Tide
ideas.description: A coast where the tides are controlled by an enormous clockwork mechanism built into the cliff — gears exposed to the weather, wound each morning by a keeper with a giant key.

ideas.title: The Afternoon Made of Gold
ideas.description: A single long summer afternoon that is so golden it leaves stains — on the grass, on the skin of people in it, on their memories. Years later they still find traces of that particular light.

ideas.title: The Weight of Blue
ideas.description: A scientist studying the physical weight of different blues — the heaviness of midnight blue versus the lightness of sky blue, the particular heft of the sea on a grey day.

ideas.title: The Cloud Archive
ideas.description: A library where every cloud formation ever recorded is kept — daguerreotypes of Victorian cumulus, painted stratus from Renaissance Italy, the cloud that appeared the day the war ended.

ideas.title: The Bioluminescent Letters
ideas.description: A coastline where the bioluminescent organisms form letters in the breaking waves — brief phosphorescent words in the dark water, different each night, in languages no shore-side scholar can identify.

ideas.title: The Portrait Painter of Clouds
ideas.description: An artist who paints formal portraits of clouds — each cloud sitting still (briefly) while the painter captures their exact expression, the particular mood of their formation, their dignity.

ideas.title: The Lighthouse of Lost Sounds
ideas.description: A lighthouse that emits sounds instead of light — broadcasting extinct bird calls, lost musical traditions, the voices of languages no one speaks. Ships navigate by it out of habit and grief.

ideas.title: The Translator of Bird Songs
ideas.description: A linguist who has devoted her life to translating birdsong — and discovered that sparrows argue about weather, crows narrate history, and nightingales compose complicated ongoing serials.

ideas.title: The Mapmaker's Last Map
ideas.description: An aged mapmaker drawing their final map — not of any place in the world, but of themselves. Mountains of old resolve, rivers of kindness, a small lake of regrets, a harbor full of names.

ideas.title: The Night That Lasted a Painting
ideas.description: A night so long and beautiful it was painted in real time — the artist chasing it hour by hour, the canvas growing rightward as more dark sky appeared, dawn finally outrunning the brushes.

ideas.title: The Terrarium of Moments
ideas.description: A glass terrarium containing living moments — a first handshake still happening in miniature, a laugh still ringing, an embrace still mid-embrace. Tended daily with careful watering.

ideas.title: The Cartographer of Grief
ideas.description: A hand-drawn map of grief — the first days a great featureless grey plain, then gradually a topography emerges: hills of good days, the recurring river, the eventual small harbor.

ideas.title: The Bakery That Opens at 4am
ideas.description: A bakery that opens at 4am, just for the night workers, the insomniacs, the early mourners. The bread is different at that hour — denser with quiet, the crust a shade darker.

ideas.title: The Island of Misremembered Summers
ideas.description: An island you can only find if you follow a summer you half-remember — the one that might have been the best one, or might not have happened quite that way. The island is warm either way.

ideas.title: The Ornithologist of Impossible Birds
ideas.description: A scientist who studies birds that shouldn't exist — the Grief-Finch, the Nostalgia Swift, the Dream-Petrel. She has extensive field notes and a complete illustrated guide.

ideas.title: The Forest That Migrates
ideas.description: A forest that, every hundred years, lifts its roots and walks slowly to a new location — leaving cleared land dotted with perfect root-holes, arriving somewhere that needed trees.

ideas.title: The Harbour of Almost-Departures
ideas.description: A harbour full of ships that almost left — boats permanently mid-departure, gangplanks half-raised, ropes half-untied, passengers looking back at a shore they couldn't quite leave.

ideas.title: The Snow Geologist
ideas.description: A geologist studying the layers of snow like geological strata — each layer a different storm, a different year. The oldest layer at the bottom is a snowstorm from 1847, still perfectly cold.

ideas.title: The Collector of First Words
ideas.description: A linguist who travels the world collecting the very first word spoken by each language — tiny audio recordings in clay jars, labeled by language and date. The oldest jar is 8,000 years old.

ideas.title: The Roof Garden of a Skyscraper in Winter
ideas.description: A rooftop garden 80 floors up in deep winter — frost-covered vegetables, pigeons huddled under kale, a small snow-dusted shed where someone grows tomatoes against all reason, city spread below.

ideas.title: The Keeper of Unlit Candles
ideas.description: A caretaker who tends thousands of unlit candles in a vast dark warehouse — never lighting them, only dusting, replacing, keeping them ready. They are for the moment everyone decides at once.

ideas.title: The Museum of Doorknobs
ideas.description: A museum dedicated entirely to doorknobs from significant doors — the knob from a room where a great decision was made, from a door never opened, from a door that opened everything.

ideas.title: The Cartographer Who Drew the Ocean Floor
ideas.description: A Victorian cartographer who spent 40 years drawing the ocean floor from imagination alone — and was later found to be almost entirely correct. The mountains are in the right places. The abysses too.

ideas.title: The Afternoon in the Amber
ideas.description: A summer afternoon caught in a piece of amber — the light still warm inside, a dragonfly mid-hover, the particular quality of 3pm in July, preserved for 65 million years.

ideas.title: The Storm Cellar of Feelings
ideas.description: A storm cellar where the owner has stored decades of difficult feelings — lined up in jars like preserves, labeled with dates and context. They visit sometimes, just to check things are still there.

ideas.title: The Postal Route Through the Mountains
ideas.description: A postal route so remote it is still served on horseback — one rider, six mountain passes, letters that take three weeks to arrive, still sealed with wax, always exactly delivered.

ideas.title: The Ghost of Summer Past
ideas.description: The ghost of a particularly good summer haunting a house — the scent of sunscreen in winter rooms, warmth on a cold staircase where someone once stood in sunlight, the echo of a garden party.

ideas.title: The Painter of Invisible Things
ideas.description: An artist who paints only things that cannot be seen — wind direction, the weight of an apology, the texture of embarrassment, the particular shape of missing someone. Sold out every show.

ideas.title: The Coral Reef at Midnight
ideas.description: A coral reef that transforms completely at midnight — daytime creatures retreat, the night colony emerges, bioluminescent polyps open, and the whole reef glows in colours unseen by day.

ideas.title: The Giant's Rocking Chair
ideas.description: An enormous rocking chair in a mountain meadow — weathered, mossy, enormous — where a giant once sat. Still rocking, very slowly, from the momentum of when they last rose, centuries ago.

ideas.title: The Storm Diarist
ideas.description: A meteorologist who writes diary entries as if the storms are people — 'She arrived at 4pm, exhausted from the Atlantic. She brought heavy rain but her lightning was controlled, almost careful.'

ideas.title: Planetarium for One
ideas.description: A portable planetarium — a small dome that unfolds in any room, projecting a private cosmos on the ceiling. You can change the date to any night in history and be under that exact sky.

ideas.title: The River of Intentions
ideas.description: A river flowing not with water but with intentions — the deep current carries the strong intentions, the surface sparkles with fragile ones. The abandoned intentions pool in eddies and moss over.

ideas.title: The Collector of Unfinished Sketches
ideas.description: An artist who collects other artists' unfinished sketches — the half-drawn bird, the portrait abandoned mid-eye, the landscape without its sky. She finds them more alive than anything completed.

ideas.title: The Underground River of Light
ideas.description: A subterranean river made of light — bending through caves in luminous curves, refracting off underground crystals, fish made of photons darting in bright schools through the dark rock.

ideas.title: The Cartographer of Sleep
ideas.description: A sleep scientist who maps the territory of different people's sleeps — the vast open plains of deep dreamless sleep, the crowded city of REM, the border country of half-waking.

ideas.title: The Paper Boat Harbour
ideas.description: A harbour entirely for paper boats — the serious origami frigate, the hastily torn receipt boat, the lovingly folded newspaper vessel. Each has log books written in pencil, ports of call imagined.

ideas.title: The Archive of Sky
ideas.description: A vast storage facility containing every photographed sky from every day since photography was invented — an unbroken record of the sky above every city, organized by cloud type and year.

ideas.title: The Last Keeper of Snowflakes
ideas.description: A scientist who catches individual snowflakes and preserves them under cold glass — each one photographed, catalogued, named. The collection covers 40 winters. No two are the same.

ideas.title: The House that Remembers Everyone
ideas.description: An old house that has absorbed the memories of all who lived there — the wallpaper holds conversations, the stairs remember different footsteps, the kitchen still smells of someone's grandmother.

ideas.title: The Moth Observatory
ideas.description: An observatory built not for stars but for moths — giant lenses for watching their flight patterns, recording their responses to different lights, translating their wing-language over generations.

ideas.title: Rain Garden in a Dream
ideas.description: A garden that exists only in dreams and grows only during dreamed rain — the flowers are colours that don't exist in waking life, the soil is made of pressed memory, the rain sounds like your name.

ideas.title: The Polar Flower
ideas.description: A single flower growing in the deep arctic — it shouldn't survive but does, its petals made thicker each year, adapting, finding sunlight at its own angle, blooming briefly every June.

ideas.title: The Fog Census
ideas.description: A government department that counts the fog — fog density, fog hours, fog types, fog opinions. Their annual report is the most beautiful government document ever produced, printed on vellum.

ideas.title: The Conservatory in Winter
ideas.description: A glass conservatory in deep winter — inside: tropical heat, humidity, enormous plants pressing against the glass; outside: snow against the panes, frost patterns fighting the warmth.

ideas.title: The Geologist Who Studies Hearts
ideas.description: A geologist who applies her expertise to human hearts — identifying sedimentary layers of long relationships, igneous rock of sudden love, fossils of people once important, fault lines of old pain.

ideas.title: The Town That Runs on Candlelight
ideas.description: A small town that refuses electricity, lit entirely by candles — every window warm and flickering, the streets navigated by lamplight, every face softened by the logic of flame.

ideas.title: Portrait of Rain on a Window
ideas.description: An extreme close-up illustration of rain on glass — each drop a lens showing a distorted street below, some drops joining, the collective making a map of the world outside, inverted and scattered.

ideas.title: The Forest of Kept Promises
ideas.description: A forest where each tree represents a kept promise — the oldest trees are centuries old, their roots deep, their canopies wide. Small new saplings grow at the edges every season.

ideas.title: The Star Diver
ideas.description: An athlete who dives into star clusters rather than water — the entry point a shimmer in the night sky, the plunge through warm stellar gas, emerging starlit on the other side.

ideas.title: The Cartographer's Apprentice
ideas.description: A young apprentice learning to draw maps — making small errors that become beautiful: a mountain placed too far west, a river that meanders more than it should, a coast full of bays.

ideas.title: The Sundial's Shadow in Winter
ideas.description: A sundial in deep winter, its shadow fallen at angles it never reaches in summer — reading times the sundial has no name for, hours between what's numbered, measuring the unnamed.

ideas.title: The Night Shepherd
ideas.description: A shepherd of nights — herding darknesses of different depths from one horizon to another, keeping the black nights separate from the blue-grey ones, ensuring each city gets its proper dark.

ideas.title: The Collector of Sunrises
ideas.description: A traveler who has spent forty years chasing sunrises — rising before dawn in 200 countries, each one documented in watercolor, the collection a complete atlas of how light begins.

ideas.title: The Tide Table of Feelings
ideas.description: A scientific tide table but for emotional states — predicted high waters of joy, the low tides of late afternoon, warning notices for approaching waves of nostalgia, sunset grief advisories.

ideas.title: The Old Bell That Rings Tomorrow
ideas.description: A church bell that, due to a mysterious warping of time, always rings twelve hours before it should — waking people at midnight, announcing noon at dawn. No one has fixed it. They rely on it.

ideas.title: The Migration of Candles
ideas.description: Each autumn, unlit candles migrate southward — found in drawers, taken from shelves, moving in small wavering lines toward warmer rooms. No one mentions it. Everyone prepares for winter.

ideas.title: The One Who Stays While Everyone Leaves
ideas.description: A figure standing at a station long after the last train — watching the platform lights go out one by one, the departure boards go blank, the café chairs stacked. Still there. Still bright.
